title: RapidMeta Oncology | AR Pathway Inhibitors in Non-Metastatic CRPC v1.0
hero_h2: AR Pathway Inhibitors (Apalutamide / Enzalutamide / Darolutamide) in Non-Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer

pico.pop: Men with non-metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (PSA-only progression on ADT, PSA doubling time <=10 months, no detectable metastases on conventional imaging)
pico.int: Second-generation androgen-receptor pathway inhibitor on top of continued ADT: apalutamide 240 mg PO QD (SPARTAN), enzalutamide 160 mg PO QD (PROSPER), or darolutamide 600 mg PO BID (ARAMIS)
pico.out: Metastasis-free survival (MFS; primary in all 3 trials, defined as time to radiographic distant metastasis or death)
pico.subgroup: PSA doubling time, baseline PSA, prior ADT duration, age, ECOG, geography

real_data_entries.name: SPARTAN
real_data_entries.group: Apalutamide 240 mg PO QD vs placebo, on top of continued ADT in nmCRPC; primary endpoint MFS

allOutcomes.title: Metastasis-free survival (primary; median 40.5 mo apa vs 16.2 mo placebo)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Smith MR et al. NEJM 2018;378:1408-1418 (SPARTAN apalutamide).

real_data_entries.name: PROSPER
real_data_entries.group: Enzalutamide 160 mg PO QD vs placebo, on top of continued ADT in nmCRPC; primary endpoint MFS

allOutcomes.title: Metastasis-free survival (primary; median 36.6 mo enz vs 14.7 mo placebo)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Hussain M et al. NEJM 2018;378:2465-2474 (PROSPER enzalutamide).

real_data_entries.name: ARAMIS
real_data_entries.group: Darolutamide 600 mg PO BID vs placebo, on top of continued ADT in nmCRPC; primary endpoint MFS

allOutcomes.title: Metastasis-free survival (primary; median 40.4 mo darol vs 18.4 mo placebo)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Fizazi K et al. NEJM 2019;380:1235-1246 (ARAMIS darolutamide).
